class.name: LineMesh
class.description:
A mesh representing a 3D line.
LineMesh is used by Line3D to render lines in 3D space. It supports various options for customizing the appearance of the line, including width, color, gradient, and alignment.
You can create and modify a LineMesh directly using its methods, or use it indirectly by adding points to a Line3D node.

method.name: add_point
method.description: Adds a point to the line at the specified position. If index is -1, the point is added at the end of the line.

method.name: clear_points
method.description: Removes all points from the line.

method.name: get_point_position
method.description: Returns the position of the point at the specified index.

method.name: get_transform
method.description: Returns the transform applied to the line.

method.name: get_use_transform
method.description: Returns whether the transform is applied to the line.

method.name: redraw
method.description:
Rebuilds the mesh. Call this method after making multiple changes to the line to update its appearance.
Note: When using a Line3D node, you typically do not need to call this method directly, as the Line3D node will handle updating the mesh automatically.

method.name: remove_point
method.description: Removes the point at the specified index.

method.name: set_point_position
method.description: Sets the position of the point at the specified index.

method.name: set_transform
method.description: Sets the transform to be applied to the line.

method.name: set_use_transform
method.description: Sets whether to apply the transform to the line.

members.member: The alignment mode for the line segments.
How smooth the line caps are (higher values result in smoother caps).
Whether the line is closed (i.e., the last point connects back to the first point).
The base color of the line.
How smooth the line corners are (higher values result in smoother corners).
Whether to draw rounded caps at the ends of the line.
Whether to draw rounded corners at the points where the line changes direction.
A gradient to apply along the length of the line.
The normal vector used when alignment is set to ALIGN_TO_NORMAL.
The array of points defining the line.
Whether to simplify the line by removing unnecessary points.
The tolerance used when simplifying the line. Higher values result in more aggressive simplification.
A curve to modulate the width of the line along its length.

constants.constant: Line segments face toward the position provided.
Line segments align to the provided normal.
